Valspar R3 underway — Masters in 19 days

tennis.result: Sinner wins Miami Open 6-4 6-4 vs Lehecka — completes Sunshine Double without dropping a set
tennis.note: First since Federer (2017) · Sabalenka sweeps IW + Miami on WTA side

golf.result: Valspar Championship R3 underway — Blanchet -7 leads at Innisbrook
golf.note: Final round tomorrow · Masters at Augusta April 9–12

nascar.result: Tyler Reddick leads Cup Series with 3 wins — Darlington race tomorrow (Mar 22)
nascar.note: Reddick won COTA for historic 3rd straight to open season

topStories.headline: Vinícius sinks Man City, Salah reaches 50 — the Champions League QF field is set
body: Real Madrid knocked out Manchester City with a 2–1 win in the second leg (5–1 on aggregate) as Vinícius Júnior scored both goals and looked unstoppable throughout. It's another Champions League exit for a City side that increasingly looks like a team past its window. Meanwhile, Mohamed Salah scored his 50th Champions League goal in Liverpool's ruthless 4–0 demolition of Galatasaray. Arsenal eliminated Bayer Leverkusen 3–1 on aggregate, and PSG swept Chelsea 8–2 over two legs.
The quarterfinal draw is set: Real Madrid vs. Bayern Munich and PSG vs. Liverpool headline the ties. First legs are April 7–8.

topStories.headline: Sinner does what no man has done since Federer — wins Indian Wells and Miami without dropping a set
body: Jannik Sinner defeated Jiří Lehečka 6–4, 6–4 in the Miami Open final to complete the 'Sunshine Double' — winning both Indian Wells and Miami in the same season without dropping a set. He's the first man since Roger Federer in 2017 to pull it off. Sinner beat Daniil Medvedev at Indian Wells, then dismantled Alexander Zverev in the semis and Lehečka in the final in Miami. On the WTA side, Aryna Sabalenka also swept both hardcourt Masters events.

topStories.headline: Knicks survive the Nets 93–92 — 14 straight in the city rivalry
body: Karl-Anthony Towns had 26 points and 15 rebounds, Jalen Brunson scored 17, and New York survived Brooklyn's furious fourth-quarter comeback to win 93–92. The Knicks trailed by 13 in the first half, blew a 14-point lead in the fourth, and were saved by Ben Saraf's missed 45-foot buzzer attempt. It's the Knicks' 14th consecutive win over the Nets — the longest winning streak for either team in the city rivalry.

stories.headline: Reddick-level dominance: Hawks streak snapped, Grant hits the buzzer, Celtics roll
body: Kevin Durant (25 pts) and Jabari Smith Jr. (23 pts) ended Atlanta's 11-game winning streak as Houston rolled 117–95. Jerami Grant broke Minnesota's hearts with a three-pointer at the 22.2-second mark to give Portland a 108–104 win — the Timberwolves are now on the wrong end of the play-in bubble. Jaylen Brown was Player of the Night as Boston handled Memphis 117–112. Detroit beat Golden State 115–101; Denver downed Toronto 121–115.

stories.headline: Cunningham remains day-to-day — West chaos from 5 through 9
body: Detroit's Cade Cunningham (back spasms) remains day-to-day and the Pistons are being cautious heading into the final stretch. The Western Conference seeds 5 through 9 are separated by barely two games — Portland's win and Minnesota's loss tighten the race further. Every night in the West matters now, and the Trail Blazers are making a real push for the play-in.

stories.headline: Colorado clinches — Makar is approaching an all-time defenseman milestone
body: The Colorado Avalanche became the first team in the NHL to clinch a playoff berth on Friday, locking up a postseason spot with weeks to spare. Cale Makar is closing in on 500 career points — a mark he could reach this week that would put him among the fastest defensemen in history to hit the milestone. Colorado's dominance this season has been historic and sustained.

stories.headline: Toronto–Ottawa in a four-point Atlantic battle
body: The Maple Leafs and Senators met Friday in a meaningful Atlantic matchup with playoff seeding implications. Toronto (40–22–8) and Ottawa (37–24–9) are just four points apart in the standings, with Boston right there too. The winner of this three-way race likely earns home-ice advantage in round one — and these teams know it.

stories.headline: Four days to Opening Night — the rosters are almost set
body: MLB Opening Night is March 25 — four days away. Teams face final roster decisions this weekend. The Yankees are managing Juan Soto's return from the WBC alongside a packed outfield. The Dodgers are still sorting rotation order after WBC fatigue. The Padres are monitoring Fernando Tatis Jr.'s wrist. Spring training wraps Sunday — and the games start counting Tuesday.

stories.headline: PSG's 8–2 aggregate win over Chelsea is the statement of the round
body: Paris Saint-Germain dismantled Chelsea over two legs, advancing 8–2 on aggregate after winning the second leg 3–0. It's an utter statement of PSG's quality this season — their attack looks as deep and dangerous as any club in Europe. Combined with Arsenal's tidy 3–1 aggregate win over Leverkusen, the quarter-final field now has a genuine English presence in the Gunners and Liverpool facing a PSG side playing the best football in the competition.

stories.headline: QF preview: Real Madrid vs. Bayern is the tie of the round
body: Real Madrid vs. Bayern Munich in the Champions League quarter-final is exactly the kind of blockbuster the competition was built for. Madrid come in red-hot — Vinícius is playing some of the best football of his career, and eliminating Man City 5–1 on aggregate sends a clear message. Bayern, meanwhile, have been grinding their way through the competition. First legs on April 7–8 — mark your calendar.

stories.headline: Antonelli leads the championship after two races — Mercedes' young gun is for real
body: Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 Formula 1 World Championship after winning the Chinese Grand Prix in only his second career race, following a 2nd-place finish behind teammate George Russell in Australia. The 19-year-old Italian is already the story of the season — calm, measured, and fast enough to beat the field clean in Shanghai. Lewis Hamilton finished third in both races for a strong Ferrari start. The Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka is next weekend (March 27–29), where Antonelli will look to extend his lead.

stories.headline: Sinner's Sunshine Double is the greatest hardcourt stretch since Federer's peak
body: Jannik Sinner is simply the best player in the world right now. He won Indian Wells over Daniil Medvedev, then moved through the Miami draw without dropping a set — beating Alexander Zverev in the semis and Jiří Lehečka 6–4, 6–4 in the final. No man had completed the Sunshine Double without dropping a set since Roger Federer in 2017. Sinner is now the heavy favorite heading into the clay season. On the women's side, Aryna Sabalenka matched that feat with her own Indian Wells–Miami sweep.

stories.headline: Third round underway at Innisbrook — Masters looms in 19 days
body: Chandler Blanchet leads the Valspar Championship at –7 through 36 holes at Innisbrook's Copperhead Course. The final round is Sunday. The bigger story looming behind every March PGA Tour event: the Masters at Augusta National begins April 9. Rory McIlroy is the defending champion, seeking to become only the fourth player ever to successfully defend at Augusta and the first since Tiger Woods in 2002. Scottie Scheffler and the rest of the world's top players are tuning their games with Augusta in mind.

stories.headline: Tyler Reddick is running away with 2026 — three wins before March is over
body: Tyler Reddick became the only driver in NASCAR Cup Series history to win the first three races of a season when he took the checkered flag at Circuit of the Americas earlier this month. He now has three wins and a commanding points lead heading into Darlington Raceway tomorrow (March 22) — one of NASCAR's most demanding and historic tracks. No driver has dominated a season opening this completely in the modern era. The rest of the field is essentially playing for second.
